got response time data from an app—bimodal with a spike at zero and long right tail. mean's 45s but median's 2s, std dev huge. histogram confirms it. tried log(1+x) transform for normality but need untransformed descriptives for the report. best way to summarize central tendency + spread + shape without confusing stakeholders? IQR + mode? or something else?

building a scalable auth system for a multi-tenant SaaS SPA (React + NestJS API on k8s). JWTs for stateless access tokens make sense for horizontal scaling, but revocation on logout/suspension is a pain without a distributed blacklist eating redis/memcached. Refresh token rotation helps but introspecting with a central auth service adds latency on every renew—tradeoff vs self-contained JWTs with short expiry? OIDC integration for enterprise SSO too. Battle-tested way to minimize state while keeping blacklists lean and revoke fast?

I'm building a simple compiler backend and my register allocator is spilling too aggressively on tight loops—even with good liveness info, it evicts live temps prematurely. Tried graph coloring with heuristics but still 20% more spills than expected. Bump intervals or go linear scan? Tradeoffs for code size vs perf?

stuck resumming the transseries for Painlevé I beyond the first instanton sector—the formal power series φ satisfies [φ] = 0 but Δ_1 [φ] = -6 exp(-6ζ^{5/2}/5) or whatever the scaling is, and higher aliens Δ_{k} [φ] = P_k(Δ_1[φ], ..., Δ_{k-1}[φ]) from the iteration, but for k>=3 the polynomials aren't closing under the lateral action due to log terms in the bridge equation. how do you compute the full resurgence algebra explicitly, or bootstrap the actual solution without numerically solving the ODE on every Stokes line? Écalle’s book is dense af on this.

hey, atwood machine with m1 hanging vertically, m2 on horizontal table with kinetic friction μ. FBDs give tension T same on both, accel a = (m1 g - μ m2 g) / (m1 + m2), but plugging numbers doesn't match my quick sim (python solver). friction only opposes on table right? where'd i screw up?

trying to implement a linearizable MPMC bounded deque using only Go channels + atomics (no mutexes/sync.Map), for work-stealing in a custom scheduler. Unbuffered chans give strict backpressure but livelock on steals under weak memorder (arm64 repro at GOMAXPROCS=256). Buffered blows GC at 50M ops/s. Adaptive buffer sizing via runtime/metrics? Proving ABA-free pops with chan closes as tombstones? Or is channels fundamentally wrong primitive—switch to unsafe for ringbuf? Tradeoffs for strict wait-freedom vs throughput?

trying to get heterogeneous lookup working in std::pmr::unordered_map<string_view, shared_ptr<cache_entry>> for a 10M-item LRU cache—no string copies on insert/find/erase. transparent hash/eq with std::hash<string_view> + string_view::compare(), but erase(view) mismatches buckets post-rehash since key_type is pmr::string and views don't alloc. insert_or_assign(view, ...) allocates fine but lookup chains to string ctor anyway on collision.

gcc 13 does the dance but 15-25% perf regression vs homogeneous on hot paths (probes deep); MSVC 19.3 asserts on erase. custom key_equal that forwards to pmr::string::compare without materializing? or override rehash hooks? abseil flat_hash_map sidesteps but kills pmr interop. sticking std C++20—any solid workaround preserving O(1) amortized + exception safety, or am I missing a traits extension?

trying to compute the minimal polynomial of a large sparse matrix over Q—factor the char poly first (slow for deg 100) or use Krylov lanczos on powers till linear dep? tradeoffs for exactness vs speed, any good sage/mpmath tricks?
